powerwashBeginnerGuide.title: Start Cleaning Faster in PowerWash Simulator 2
powerwashBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the cleaning loop, nozzle roles, free soap system and the best route through the opening jobs. Use the task list and dirt highlight to finish jobs without hunting blindly for the final specks.
powerwashBeginnerGuide.intro: Career Mode contains 38 new jobs across Muckingham and the wider Caldera County. Jobs pay Cash for washers and attachments, while Career and Free Play also award PowerWash Points for furniture and cosmetic customization at the Home Base.

steps.title: Set up at the Home Base
steps.description: Use the Job Map to choose Career assignments, visit the shop before leaving and return to the Home Base to clean and place unlocked furniture. Career jobs are the main source of Cash for new washers and equipment.
steps.quickTip: Spend early Cash on tools that improve coverage before buying cosmetic items.

steps.title: Learn the core cleaning controls
steps.description: Practice holding or toggling the washer, switching washers, opening the nozzle and extension wheels, rotating the nozzle, opening the task list, crouching, jumping and activating the dirt highlight. The task list shows which named parts are still incomplete.
steps.quickTip: Use continuous washing for long flat passes and free aim for edges, undersides and awkward angles.

steps.title: Match the nozzle to the surface
steps.description: The 40 and 25 nozzles cover broad, lightly soiled areas; the 15 and 0 nozzles concentrate pressure on narrow seams and stubborn dirt. Turbo attacks small stubborn patches, while the Adaptable Nozzle lets you change spray width without swapping attachments.
steps.quickTip: Start wide, then narrow the spray only when a surface stops cleaning efficiently.

steps.title: Clean the Removals Van in sections
steps.description: Begin with the roof and upper panels, sweep across both sides, clean the front and rear, then finish wheels, steps, handles and the underside. This is the opening Career job and can be completed with the free Prime Vista 1500 or Urban X U1.
steps.quickTip: Complete large panels before small trim so the task list becomes shorter and easier to scan.

steps.title: Understand multi-stage jobs
steps.description: The Public Facility introduces areas that reveal themselves in stages. Clean every currently available task to trigger the next section, then repeat the same top-to-bottom routine inside the newly opened area.
steps.quickTip: Do not waste time searching behind locked or hidden sections; finish the visible stage first.

steps.title: Use Soap 2.0 on stubborn layers
steps.description: Soap is free to use and clings to dirty surfaces. Cover a heavily soiled section with the Soap Nozzle, then rinse it with any water nozzle to remove the softened grime faster.
steps.quickTip: Soap broad dirty zones first, but use focused water nozzles for tiny missed edges.

steps.title: Buy coverage upgrades first
steps.description: The Adaptable Nozzle is the most flexible early attachment, while the SwirlForce Surf Ace is designed for floors and other large flat surfaces. Extensions, ladders, scaffolding, the scissor lift and abseiling kit solve height and reach problems in later jobs.
steps.quickTip: Choose equipment for the next job's shape: surface cleaner for flat areas, extensions for roofs and focused nozzles for vehicles.

steps.title: Finish the final percentage quickly
steps.description: Open the task list, select an incomplete part and use dirt highlight while circling the object from multiple heights. Check undersides, rear faces, trim seams, wheel wells, roof edges and surfaces hidden behind props.
steps.quickTip: When a part is almost clean, stop spraying the whole level and inspect only that named task.

powerwashJobsAndLocations.title: All 38 PowerWash Simulator 2 Career Jobs in Unlock Order
powerwashJobsAndLocations.subtitle: Follow the full base-game campaign from the Removals Van to Mount Rushless. Each row identifies the job location, cleaning format, practical equipment choice and an achievement or completion detail.
powerwashJobsAndLocations.intro: The 38-job Career campaign spans Muckingham and the wider Caldera County, with official location highlights including Sponge Valley, Suds East and Lubri City. Later jobs make heavier use of surface cleaning, extensions, lifts and abseiling.

jobs.equipment: Prime Vista 1500 or Urban X U1; 25 for panels and 15 for seams
jobs.notableDetail: Completing this opening Career job unlocks the All Moved In achievement.

jobs.equipment: 25 or Adaptable Nozzle; soap for heavy layers
jobs.notableDetail: The structure rises from the ground, then reveals its exterior and interior as each stage is completed.

jobs.equipment: 25 for tents and furniture; 0 red nozzle for the achievement route
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the lanterns first using only the red nozzle to earn Hit the Lights.

jobs.equipment: Adaptable Nozzle, extensions and ladder access
jobs.notableDetail: Work top to bottom so runoff and missed trim are easier to track.

jobs.equipment: Soap Nozzle followed by 15 or 25
jobs.notableDetail: Cover every wash task with soap at the same time to earn Doggy Bath.

jobs.equipment: SwirlForce Surf Ace for broad flat areas; abseiling rig for height
jobs.notableDetail: The surface cleaner is especially effective on the large sign face and lettering.

jobs.location: Great River National Park, Power Falls
jobs.equipment: Adaptable Nozzle and soap
jobs.notableDetail: Place a gnome on each seat to earn Tea Time.

jobs.equipment: 15 or Adaptable Nozzle for moving and detailed targets
jobs.notableDetail: Complete the Bigfoot Target first to earn Where's Woolly?.

jobs.equipment: 15 for mechanisms, 25 for body panels, soap for stubborn grime
jobs.notableDetail: Use the task list to isolate wheels, brushes and underside parts near the end.

jobs.equipment: SwirlForce Surf Ace for paving; Adaptable Nozzle for pumps and signs
jobs.notableDetail: Roll the ball down the playground slide to earn Drop the Ball.

jobs.equipment: Surface cleaner for the floor; Adaptable Nozzle for rails and trim
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the central hatch last to earn Hatching a Plan.

jobs.equipment: 15 or Adaptable Nozzle
jobs.notableDetail: Its compact size makes it a practical replay job for nozzle-only achievements.

jobs.equipment: Adaptable Nozzle, long extension and scaffolding
jobs.notableDetail: Divide the exterior into walls, roofline, windows and fixtures before the final dirt scan.

jobs.equipment: Abseiling kit, extensions and Adaptable Nozzle
jobs.notableDetail: Use traversal equipment to clean elevated bodywork without repeatedly repositioning from the ground.

jobs.equipment: Turbo Nozzle and soap for the drum and chassis
jobs.notableDetail: Rotate around the vehicle and check the drum rim, frame and wheel recesses separately.

jobs.equipment: Surface cleaner for broad ground areas; extensions for structures
jobs.notableDetail: Clear large flat areas first, then finish fences, machinery and narrow edges.

jobs.location: Great River National Park, Power Falls
jobs.equipment: 25 or 40 for stone faces; soap for layered dirt
jobs.notableDetail: Stand on every pillar without touching the ground to earn Floor is Lava.

jobs.equipment: Abseiling kit and Adaptable Nozzle
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the Pterodactyl first to earn Flying Start.

jobs.equipment: Surface cleaner for the floor; extensions for overhead fixtures
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the disco ball first to earn Make it all Shine.

jobs.equipment: Turbo or 15 Nozzle; soap around the engine and tires
jobs.notableDetail: Check the axle, tread grooves and underside before relying on the final dirt highlight.

jobs.equipment: Adaptable Nozzle, soap and extensions
jobs.notableDetail: Descend the stairs during the job to earn Deep Lore.

jobs.equipment: Extensions for the roof; 15 or Adaptable Nozzle for details
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the satellite dish first to earn Message Received.

jobs.equipment: Surface cleaner for aisles and stage; extensions for upper details
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the entire front seating row first to earn Front Row Seats.

jobs.equipment: Scissor lift, extensions and Adaptable Nozzle
jobs.notableDetail: Clean all room doors, door handles and door numbers first to earn Door to Door.

jobs.equipment: Adaptable Nozzle and Turbo Nozzle for track hardware
jobs.notableDetail: Ride the roller coaster for a full loop to earn Ticket to Ride.

jobs.equipment: 15 or Adaptable Nozzle for models and supports
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the Sun, then every planet in order moving outward to earn Cosmic Ordering.

jobs.equipment: 15 and 25 Nozzles; soap for layered exterior grime
jobs.notableDetail: Work one side at a time, then inspect the roofline, wheels and undercarriage.

jobs.location: Great River National Park, Power Falls
jobs.equipment: Surface cleaner, extensions and soap
jobs.notableDetail: Use room-by-room or wall-by-wall passes to keep the large task list manageable.

jobs.equipment: Heavy- or professional-duty washer with Turbo Nozzle
jobs.notableDetail: Prioritize the enormous bed and tires, then inspect ladders, rails and hydraulic parts.

jobs.equipment: Surface cleaner and Adaptable Nozzle
jobs.notableDetail: Play the giant piano from low to high for Glissando!, or clean a rotating floor disc with the surface cleaner for Spin Doctor.

jobs.equipment: Scissor lift, long extensions and professional-duty washer
jobs.notableDetail: Clean broad exterior surfaces first, then use the lift for roofline and elevated fittings.

jobs.equipment: 15 and 25 Nozzles with soap
jobs.notableDetail: Split the long body into front, middle and rear sections to avoid losing track of small trim pieces.

jobs.equipment: Surface cleaner, scissor lift and professional-duty washer
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the owl statue first to earn Wise Decision.

jobs.equipment: 0 or 15 for tight components; soap for dense grime
jobs.notableDetail: Use short, controlled passes around the wheels, frame joints and compact mechanical parts.

jobs.equipment: Adaptable Nozzle, extensions and soap
jobs.notableDetail: Treat each set as a separate mini-job and finish its props before moving to the next.

jobs.equipment: Professional-duty washer, Turbo Nozzle and long extension
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the trailer panels in broad passes, then isolate the cab, wheels and undercarriage.

jobs.equipment: Adaptable Nozzle and soap
jobs.notableDetail: Clean the mouse last to earn Squeaky Clean.

jobs.equipment: Professional-duty washer plus all available traversal tools
jobs.notableDetail: The final Career job is the largest capstone clean; use the task list and dirt highlight frequently during the last percentages.

powerwashBestEquipment.title: Best PowerWash Simulator 2 Washers, Nozzles and Tools
powerwashBestEquipment.subtitle: Compare the Light, Medium, Heavy and Professional washer lines, plus the SwirlForce Surf Ace and specialized nozzles. Rankings prioritize real cleaning speed, coverage, reach and value during Career progression.
powerwashBestEquipment.intro: PowerWash Simulator 2 offers nine different Prime Vista and Urban X washers across its Career progression, plus the SwirlForce Surf Ace surface cleaner. Standard washer options include the core 0, 15, 25, 40, Turbo and Soap nozzles, while the Adaptable Nozzle can be purchased for every washer.

items.name: Prime Vista Pro + Triple-Tip Nozzle
items.bestFor: Large late-game jobs, mixed surfaces and fast general cleaning
items.why: The Prime Vista Pro combines professional pressure with the exclusive Triple-Tip Nozzle, giving it exceptional coverage without losing too much cleaning force.

items.name: Urban X Stream + Double Turbo Nozzle
items.bestFor: Heavy grime, vehicles, machinery and narrow high-pressure passes
items.why: The Urban X Stream gains the exclusive Double Turbo Nozzle, making it one of the strongest options for concentrated cleaning.

items.name: Adaptable Nozzle
items.bestFor: Switching between broad panels and narrow details without changing nozzles
items.why: It is purchasable for every washer and lets the player manually change spray width, making it the most flexible upgrade in the game.

items.name: SwirlForce Surf Ace + 20-inch Head
items.bestFor: Floors, paving, stages, wide walls and other broad flat areas
items.why: The rotating surface cleaner covers large areas quickly, and the 20-inch head provides its widest cleaning path.

items.name: Prime Vista 3000
items.bestFor: Buildings, large props and long cleaning sessions
items.why: A strong late-career workhorse that improves pressure and range before the professional tier becomes affordable.

items.name: Urban X MAX
items.bestFor: Vehicles, stubborn dirt and compact mechanical details
items.why: The heavy-duty Urban X option favors aggressive cleaning and performs well with Turbo and Adaptable attachments.

items.name: Soap Nozzle
items.bestFor: Stubborn stains and heavily layered dirt
items.why: Soap is free, clings visibly to the surface and allows any water nozzle to rinse softened grime away.

items.name: Turbo Nozzle
items.bestFor: Rust-like patches, tire grooves, machinery and tight stubborn spots
items.why: Its rotating concentrated spray removes stubborn dirt faster than broad fan nozzles, but covers less area.

items.name: Prime Vista 2400
items.bestFor: Houses, attractions and general-purpose Career jobs
items.why: A balanced upgrade from the starter tier with enough pressure and reach for most early and mid-game work.

items.name: Urban X U2
items.why: A practical mid-game Urban X washer for players who prefer stronger focused cleaning over maximum reach.

items.name: Prime Vista 1500
items.bestFor: The Removals Van, Public Facility and other opening jobs
items.why: It is available free at the start and remains useful when paired with the Adaptable Nozzle.

items.name: Urban X U1
items.bestFor: Compact vehicles, trim and close-range cleaning
items.why: The second free starter washer provides a different handling option before the medium-duty tier is purchased.

powerwashMultiplayer.title: PowerWash Simulator 2 Multiplayer, Crossplay and Split-Screen
powerwashMultiplayer.subtitle: Play Career or Free Play with up to four people online, or clean locally with two players on one screen. Platform subscriptions and crossplay availability differ between Xbox, PC, PS5 and Nintendo Switch 2.
powerwashMultiplayer.intro: PowerWash Simulator 2 adds shared online campaign progression and two-player local split-screen. Online sessions support up to four players in Career and Free Play, while cross-platform play is available between supported Xbox and PC versions rather than across every platform.

faqs.question: How many players can play PowerWash Simulator 2 together?
faqs.answer: Online co-op supports up to four players. Local split-screen supports two players on one PC or console, and the game can also be played solo.

faqs.question: Which PowerWash Simulator 2 modes support online co-op?
faqs.answer: Both Career and Free Play support four-player online co-op. Online teams share job progression during the session, so large jobs can be completed together instead of each player repeating the same clean separately.

faqs.question: How does local split-screen work in PowerWash Simulator 2?
faqs.answer: Open the multiplayer menu and start a split-screen session, then connect a second controller or local input supported by the platform. Two players clean the same job from one screen and contribute to the same campaign save.

faqs.question: Does PowerWash Simulator 2 support crossplay?
faqs.answer: Crossplay is supported between Xbox Series X|S and compatible PC versions, including Steam, Epic Games Store and Windows Store. PS5 players use the PlayStation player pool, while Nintendo Switch 2 players use the Switch 2 player pool.

faqs.question: How do I host or join an online PowerWash Simulator 2 session?
faqs.answer: Open the multiplayer screen from the pause menu, change Session Type from Singleplayer to Online Multiplayer and share the generated Room Code. Joining players open the same menu, choose Enter a Code and type the host's code; platform invites can also be used where supported.

faqs.question: What subscriptions are required for PowerWash Simulator 2 console online play?
faqs.answer: PS5 online multiplayer requires PlayStation Plus. Nintendo Switch 2 online play requires Nintendo Switch Online. Xbox console online multiplayer requires an eligible Xbox Game Pass tier that includes online console multiplayer. PC online play does not require a console subscription.

faqs.question: Do PowerWash Simulator 2 career progress and saves transfer between platforms?
faqs.answer: Shared online progression lets a co-op team advance a job together, but the game does not provide cross-platform save transfer. A save created on one platform ecosystem does not automatically move to another ecosystem.

faqs.question: Can completed PowerWash Simulator 2 jobs be replayed with friends?
faqs.answer: Yes. Completed Career jobs can be selected again in Free Play, and online co-op supports Free Play with up to four players. This is useful for achievement routes, nozzle-only runs and relaxed replays.

powerwashAchievements.title: All PowerWash Simulator 2 Achievements and Trophies
powerwashAchievements.subtitle: Track all 60 current achievements across the base game and both paid packs. Every entry includes its exact requirement, related job, difficulty and a fast completion method.
powerwashAchievements.intro: The current list contains 40 base-game achievements, 10 Adventure Time achievements and 10 STAR WARS achievements. Clear normal completion milestones during the campaigns, then replay jobs for clean-first, clean-last, equipment-only and hidden interaction challenges.

entries.requirement: Complete the Removals Van job in Career Mode.
entries.guide: Finish the opening Career job normally; the achievement unlocks when the job completion screen appears.

entries.requirement: Complete a wash task while abseiling.
entries.job: Any job with an abseiling rig
entries.guide: Attach to the abseiling rig, remain suspended and finish one named wash task to 100%.

entries.requirement: In the Home Base, select a job using the Job Map.
entries.guide: Use the large Job Map in the Home Base and launch any available assignment from it.

entries.requirement: In the Home Base, pet all three cats.
entries.guide: Find each of the three cats around the Home Base and use the interaction prompt on every cat once.

entries.requirement: In the Home Base, clean and place a piece of furniture.
entries.guide: Choose a dirty furniture item, wash it to 100%, then place it through the Home Base decoration controls.

entries.requirement: Equip an item of clothing.
entries.guide: Open character customization and equip any owned clothing item.

entries.requirement: Own a light duty washer and all of its attachments at the same time.
entries.guide: Buy the light duty washer and every compatible attachment, keeping the complete set in your inventory.

entries.requirement: Own the surface cleaner and all of its attachments at the same time.
entries.guide: Purchase the surface cleaner and every attachment made for it without refunding any part of the set.

entries.requirement: In the Temple Interior job, descend the stairs.
entries.guide: Progress through the job until the stairway is accessible, then walk down it.

entries.requirement: In the Home Base, examine an item on Harper's pinboard.
entries.guide: Approach Harper's pinboard and interact with any pinned note or item.

entries.requirement: Apply a skin to your Van.
entries.guide: Open the Van appearance options and apply any unlocked skin.

entries.requirement: Own a medium duty washer and all of its attachments at the same time.
entries.guide: Buy the medium duty washer and every compatible attachment, then keep the full set owned together.

entries.requirement: Own a professional duty washer and all of its attachments at the same time.
entries.guide: Save enough cash to purchase the professional duty washer and every compatible attachment without refunding them.

entries.achievement: It All Comes Out in the Wash
entries.requirement: Complete all jobs in Career Mode.
entries.guide: Finish every Career assignment, including all stages of multi-stage jobs, until the Career list is fully complete.

entries.requirement: In the Dog Car job, have soap on every wash task at the same time.
entries.guide: Cover every listed wash task with soap before fully rinsing any one task clean.

entries.requirement: Own a heavy duty washer and all of its attachments at the same time.
entries.guide: Purchase the heavy duty washer and all compatible attachments, keeping the complete set in your inventory.

entries.requirement: Complete any job using only the Adaptable Nozzle.
entries.guide: Choose a compact job, equip the Adaptable Nozzle from the start and do not switch to another nozzle or cleaner.

entries.requirement: In the Home Base, find and pick up the hidden gnome.
entries.guide: Search the Home Base rooms and corners for the concealed gnome, then use the interaction prompt to pick it up.

entries.requirement: In the Mini Roller Coaster job, ride the Roller Coaster for a full loop.
entries.guide: Board the moving coaster and stay on it until it completes one uninterrupted circuit.

entries.requirement: Apply a skin to a Washer.
entries.guide: Open washer customization and apply any owned washer skin.

entries.requirement: In the Teapot Tea Room job, place a gnome on each seat.
entries.guide: Move the available gnomes onto the seats and confirm that every seat has one before completing the job.

entries.requirement: Own all washers and attachments at the same time.
entries.guide: Accumulate enough money to own every washer and every attachment simultaneously; avoid refunding earlier equipment.

entries.requirement: In the Gas Station job, roll the ball down the Playground Slide.
entries.guide: Use the water stream to push the loose ball onto the top of the Playground Slide and send it all the way down.

entries.requirement: In the Fun House job, hit all Giant Piano keys in order from low to high.
entries.guide: Start on the lowest Giant Piano key and spray each next key in ascending pitch without skipping or reversing the order.

entries.requirement: Complete any job using only the Prime Vista Triple-Tip nozzle.
entries.guide: Equip the Prime Vista Triple-Tip nozzle at the start of a small job and keep it equipped through 100% completion.

entries.requirement: In any job, fully extend a Scissor Lift with a gnome on it.
entries.job: Any job with a Scissor Lift
entries.guide: Place a movable gnome on the lift platform, then raise the Scissor Lift to its maximum height.

entries.requirement: In the Rock Climbing Park job, clean the Pterodactyl first.
entries.guide: Locate the Pterodactyl and complete it before allowing any other named wash task to reach 100%.

entries.requirement: In the Shooting Gallery job, complete the Bigfoot Target first.
entries.guide: Find the Bigfoot Target and fully clean it before completing any other listed task.

entries.requirement: In the Stone Circle job, stand on all Pillars without touching the floor.
entries.guide: Plan a continuous jump route across every pillar; restart the attempt if your character touches the ground.

entries.requirement: In the Roller Disco job, clean the Disco Ball first.
entries.guide: Reach the Disco Ball and finish it before any other named object reaches 100%.

entries.requirement: In the Car and Trailer job, clean the Satellite Dish first.
entries.guide: Complete the Satellite Dish before finishing any other wash task.

entries.requirement: In the Campsite job, clean the Lanterns first using only the red nozzle.
entries.guide: Equip the red nozzle immediately and fully clean every Lantern before completing any other task or changing nozzle.

entries.requirement: In the Fun House job, clean a Rotating Floor Disc with the surface cleaner.
entries.guide: Equip the surface cleaner and keep it on one Rotating Floor Disc until that disc reaches 100%.

entries.requirement: In the Theater job, clean the entire Front Seating Row first.
entries.guide: Finish every component belonging to the Front Seating Row before completing anything outside that row.

entries.requirement: In the Bandstand job, clean the Hatch last.
entries.guide: Avoid finishing the Hatch while cleaning the rest of the Bandstand, then complete it as the final task.

entries.requirement: In the Motel job, clean all room Doors, Door Handles and Door Numbers first.
entries.guide: Work room by room and complete every Door, Door Handle and Door Number before finishing any other object.

entries.requirement: Complete any job using only the UrbanX Double Turbo Nozzle.
entries.guide: Select a short replay job, use the UrbanX Double Turbo Nozzle from the first spray and never switch tools.

entries.requirement: In the Solar System job, clean the Sun, then all planets in order from the Sun.
entries.guide: Finish the Sun first, then clean Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune in that order.

entries.requirement: In the Shopping Mall job, clean the Owl first.
entries.guide: Locate the Owl and complete it before any other named task reaches 100%.

entries.requirement: In the Town Floats job, clean the Mouse last.
entries.guide: Leave the Mouse unfinished while completing every other task, then clean the Mouse to finish the job.

entries.requirement: Complete the Living Room job.
entries.guide: Finish every wash task in the Living Room level.

entries.requirement: Complete the Pizza Sassy's job.
entries.guide: Reach 100% completion on Pizza Sassy's.

entries.requirement: Pet BMO in the Living Room job.
entries.guide: Find BMO in the Living Room and use the interaction prompt to pet them.

entries.requirement: Complete the Ice King's Castle job.
entries.guide: Finish every area and object in Ice King's Castle.

entries.requirement: Complete the Candy Vehicles job.
entries.guide: Clean all Candy Vehicles to 100%.

entries.requirement: In the Tree House job, reach maximum height on the Jake Scissor Lift.
entries.guide: Stand on or operate the Jake-themed Scissor Lift and raise it to its highest position.

entries.requirement: Complete the Tree House job.
entries.guide: Finish the full exterior Tree House assignment.

entries.requirement: Complete the Candy Vehicles job using only the Adaptable Nozzle.
entries.guide: Equip the Adaptable Nozzle before cleaning and use no other nozzle, soap applicator or surface cleaner until the job is complete.

entries.requirement: In the Ice King's Castle job, clean the Handbag last with the Cage Door closed.
entries.guide: Keep the Cage Door closed, leave the Handbag dirty while finishing everything else, then clean the Handbag as the final task.

entries.requirement: In the Pizza Sassy's job, clean all three doors first.
entries.guide: Fully clean the three door tasks before completing any other named object.

entries.achievement: What a Desolate Place This Is
entries.requirement: Complete the Lars Homestead job.
entries.guide: Finish every wash task at Lars Homestead.

entries.requirement: Complete the Mos Eisley Cantina job.
entries.guide: Reach 100% completion in the Cantina.

entries.requirement: Complete the AT-AT job.
entries.guide: Finish cleaning the full Blizzard 3 AT-AT assignment.

entries.achievement: Great Shot Kid, That Was One in a Million!
entries.requirement: In the Lars Homestead job, spray Luke's T-16 Skyhopper while standing on the EG-6 Power Droid.
entries.guide: Climb onto the EG-6 Power Droid, remain standing on it and spray Luke's T-16 Skyhopper.

entries.achievement: There is a Great Disturbance in the Force
entries.requirement: Block the MSE-6 Repair Droid from performing its patrol.
entries.guide: Stand directly in the MSE-6 Repair Droid's route and keep your character in place until its patrol is blocked.

entries.achievement: I Find Your Lack of Soap Disturbing
entries.requirement: Complete the AT-AT job without using any soap.
entries.guide: Use water nozzles and navigation equipment only; do not apply soap at any point before reaching 100%.

entries.requirement: Complete the Super Star Destroyer Bridge job.
entries.guide: Finish every bridge component and reach 100% completion.

entries.requirement: Complete the X-wing Fighter job.
entries.guide: Clean the Red 2 X-wing Fighter assignment to 100%.

entries.achievement: She's Got it Where it Counts, Kid
entries.requirement: In the Millennium Falcon job, find and clean the hidden Blaster Cannon.
entries.guide: Inspect concealed and newly exposed sections of the Falcon, locate the Blaster Cannon and clean that component fully.

entries.achievement: The Force Will Be With You, Always
entries.requirement: Complete the Millennium Falcon job.
entries.guide: Finish the entire Millennium Falcon assignment.

powerwashDlcAndUpdates.title: PowerWash Simulator 2 DLC, Free Jobs and Update Timeline
powerwashDlcAndUpdates.subtitle: Follow every major content release from the 2025 launch support plan through the latest STAR WARS Pack. The timeline separates free Caldera Chronicles jobs from paid crossover campaigns.
powerwashDlcAndUpdates.intro: FuturLab's 2025-2026 roadmap scheduled quality-of-life support, three free Caldera Chronicles jobs and three paid IP packs. Adventure Time and STAR WARS are the first two paid campaigns, while Miniature Village and Bowling Alley are already available as free jobs.

highlights: Bug fixes, optimization and quality-of-life improvements
Small furniture and cosmetic content drops
Foundation work for the larger 2026 roadmap
Added the first free Caldera Chronicles cleaning job
Added key rebinding
Added stackable furniture in the Home Base
Added controller deadzone sliders
Included job, achievement, cash tracking, navigation equipment, furniture, UI and multiplayer fixes
Five-level campaign set in the Land of Ooo
Solo campaign, shared campaign co-op and up to four players in Free Play
Interactive characters and themed equipment, including BMO and the Jake Scissor Lift
Added the second free Caldera Chronicles job
Added a message archive sortable by job and group chat
Improved clean-first and clean-last achievement unlocking
Fixed furniture placement, dirty furniture spawning, shop refunds, navigation equipment, UI and multiplayer issues
Improved cleaning behavior for the Record Player and several Home Base objects
Six jobs inspired by the original Star Wars trilogy
Play as the exclusive P0-W2 Class Five cleaning droid
New story messages from Han Solo, Luke Skywalker, Owen Lars and other characters
Star Wars-themed scissor lifts, scaffolding and abseiling rigs
Solo campaign, shared campaign co-op and up to four players in Free Play
The third free Caldera Chronicles assignment
Players can bounce higher to reach dirty elevated surfaces
Includes swinging hanging objects that must be tracked while cleaning
The third paid IP collaboration from the official 2025-2026 roadmap
Theme, job list, achievements, price and release date have not been announced

powerwashLastDirtGuide.title: How to Find the Last Dirt in PowerWash Simulator 2
powerwashLastDirtGuide.subtitle: Use the upgraded object list, orange task markers and dirt highlighting to isolate the final unfinished surfaces. This route works for vehicles, buildings and multi-stage jobs.
powerwashLastDirtGuide.intro: When a job stalls near 100%, stop washing the whole map and narrow the search to one named task at a time. The sequel lets you mark multiple incomplete sections, shows distant orange indicators and unlocks new areas during multi-stage jobs, so the fastest solution is a controlled checklist sweep rather than random spraying.

steps.title: Open the object list
steps.action: Sort or scan the job checklist for every task below 100%. Start with the item showing the lowest completion percentage because it usually has the largest visible patch remaining.
commonMisses: Tiny repeated parts listed separately
A newly added task after a multi-stage unlock
One component hidden inside a larger object

steps.title: Mark every unfinished task
steps.action: Select the incomplete checklist entries. PowerWash Simulator 2 can mark multiple unfinished sections and display orange check indicators over distant targets, making it easier to identify the correct side of a large job.
commonMisses: A matching component on the opposite side
Upper and lower versions of the same part
Several identical doors, lights, wheels or supports

steps.title: Pulse the dirt highlight
steps.action: Face the marked object and trigger the dirt highlight in short bursts while slowly rotating around it. Look for small glowing lines or dots that remain after the larger grime has disappeared.
commonMisses: Thin dirt lines along seams
Spots hidden by wet reflections
Dark grime blending into dark materials

steps.title: Switch to precision cleaning
steps.action: Put away the surface cleaner and wide spray when only a sliver remains. Use a narrow fan, the red nozzle or a narrowed Adaptable Nozzle, then trace the edge from close range.
commonMisses: Window and panel borders
Lettering, bolts and trim
Corners where two surfaces meet
The lip around circular parts

steps.title: Check every hidden angle
steps.action: Crouch, step behind the object and look upward from below. Sweep the underside first, then the rear face, inner recesses and any space partly blocked by another component.
commonMisses: Undersides of vehicles and furniture
Backs of signs and panels
Inside wheel arches, frames and railings
Door jambs, interior corners and recessed handles

steps.title: Use the correct access equipment
steps.action: Move the ladder, Scissor Lift or cherry picker until the nozzle is level with the dirty surface. On tall vertical jobs, use the abseiling rig and inspect the top edge before descending in straight passes.
commonMisses: Roof ridges and upper ledges
The tops of beams, letters and decorations
Areas directly below a lift platform
High rear surfaces visible only from outside the main route

steps.title: Recheck multi-stage sections
steps.action: After a stage completes, pause and inspect the expanded checklist before continuing. Newly opened interiors, rooms and vehicle sections add fresh tasks that may sit away from the area you were just cleaning.
commonMisses: The inside of a structure revealed after its exterior
A newly opened door, hatch or compartment
Objects that move or appear when the next stage begins
Tasks added to the bottom of the object list

steps.title: Run a final systematic sweep
steps.action: Choose one direction and finish a complete route: top to bottom, clockwise around the exterior, then through every interior room. Keep only the final incomplete task marked and repeat the edge, underside and elevation checks until the completion ding triggers.
commonMisses: Returning to random spraying instead of following the route
Leaving one side of a repeated object unchecked
Using a broad tool that passes over narrow gaps
Assuming the visible front is the whole named task

powerwashHomeBase.title: PowerWash Simulator 2 Home Base, Mementos and Collectibles
powerwashHomeBase.subtitle: Turn the Home Base into a personalized cleaning headquarters while completing its hidden interactions. Use this checklist for furniture, trinkets, job mementos, cats, cosmetics and achievement actions.
powerwashHomeBase.intro: The Home Base is a playable hub between jobs rather than a simple menu. Completed assignments add mementos, furniture can be cleaned and arranged, trinkets personalize the space, the Job Map launches work, and several small interactions unlock achievements.

items.howToGetOrUse: Approach the large map in the Home Base and select any available assignment directly from it.
items.reward: Unlocks the achievement and provides an in-world route into the job list.

items.howToGetOrUse: Collect or unlock furniture, wash dirty pieces to 100%, then position them with the Home Base decoration controls. Update 1.2 also added stackable furniture.
items.reward: Unlocks the achievement and makes the cleaned piece usable in your custom Home Base layout.

items.howToGetOrUse: Collect decorative trinkets during progression and use them to add smaller details around the Home Base.
checklist: Arrange trinkets around furniture and shelves
items.reward: Adds decorative variety to the Home Base.

items.howToGetOrUse: Complete jobs to add new mementos to the Home Base. Each memento acts as a permanent reminder of a finished cleaning assignment.
checklist: Return to the Home Base after major assignments
Check the memento display for new additions
Continue through new content to expand the collection
items.reward: Builds a visual collection that records campaign progress.

items.howToGetOrUse: Find all three cats around the Home Base and interact with each one.
items.reward: Unlocks the achievement after all three cats have been petted.

items.howToGetOrUse: Inspect Harper's pinboard and interact with any pinned note or object.
checklist: Move close enough for the prompt
items.reward: Unlocks the achievement and reveals an extra piece of Home Base detail.

items.howToGetOrUse: Search the Home Base rooms, furniture and corners until the concealed gnome can be picked up.
checklist: Inspect low shelves and floor-level hiding places
Check behind and around placed objects
items.reward: Unlocks the achievement and completes the Home Base's hidden gnome interaction.

items.howToGetOrUse: Open Van customization and apply any unlocked skin.
checklist: Apply and save the new look
items.reward: Unlocks the achievement and changes the Van's appearance.

items.howToGetOrUse: Open washer customization and apply a skin to any compatible washer.
items.reward: Unlocks the achievement and customizes the washer used on jobs.

items.howToGetOrUse: Open character customization and equip any owned clothing item.
items.reward: Unlocks the achievement and changes the cleaner's outfit.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about PowerWash Simulator 2

questions.question: What is PowerWash Simulator 2?
questions.answer: PowerWash Simulator 2 is a relaxing co-op cleaning simulator from FuturLab. You blast grime from vehicles, buildings and attractions across a 38-job Career campaign, unlock stronger washers and tools, and clean solo, online with up to four players, or in two-player local split-screen.

questions.question: Which platforms is PowerWash Simulator 2 available on?
questions.answer: PowerWash Simulator 2 is available on PC (Steam, Epic Games Store and Windows Store), PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch 2. It also launched on Xbox Game Pass on day one.

questions.question: How many jobs are in PowerWash Simulator 2?
questions.answer: The base-game PowerWash Simulator 2 Career Mode contains 38 jobs, from the opening Removals Van to the final Mount Rushless capstone. Free Caldera Chronicles jobs and paid crossover packs add even more cleaning assignments.

questions.question: Does PowerWash Simulator 2 support co-op and split-screen?
questions.answer: Yes. PowerWash Simulator 2 supports up to four players in online co-op for both Career and Free Play, plus two-player local split-screen. Crossplay is available between Xbox Series X|S and compatible PC versions.

questions.question: How many achievements does PowerWash Simulator 2 have?
questions.answer: PowerWash Simulator 2 currently has 60 achievements: 40 in the base game, 10 in the Adventure Time Pack, and 10 in the STAR WARS Pack. Each includes an exact requirement and a fast completion method.

questions.question: What DLC is available for PowerWash Simulator 2?
questions.answer: PowerWash Simulator 2 has free Caldera Chronicles jobs (Miniature Village and Bowling Alley) and two paid crossover packs: the Adventure Time Pack and the STAR WARS Pack. A third free job and a third paid IP pack are planned for later in 2026.

questions.question: Is there a Home Base in PowerWash Simulator 2?
questions.answer: Yes. The PowerWash Simulator 2 Home Base is a playable hub between jobs where you clean and arrange furniture, collect trinkets and mementos, pet cats, launch jobs from the Job Map, and unlock several achievements through hidden interactions.

questions.question: Will PowerWash Simulator 2 keep getting updates?
questions.answer: Yes. PowerWash Simulator 2's 2025-2026 roadmap from FuturLab includes free quality-of-life updates, three free Caldera Chronicles jobs, and paid crossover packs, with continued support planned throughout 2026.
